text:
Momoze Hui Guo Rou (モモゼ＝ホイコーロ, Momoze Hoikōro) was the Twelfth Prince of the Kakin Empire's fourteen Princes, first of Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou and his seventh wife, Seventh Queen Sevanti Hui Guo Rou's two children. She had one younger full brother, 13 th Prince Marayam Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Appearance

Momoze was a short young woman with delicate features and long, lustrous orange hair worn in an elaborate ponytail and black eyes. During the festival before Black Whale's departure, Momoze wore a refined long-sleeved A-line dress with a dark-colored blouse, and at the royal banquet she wore a tiara and a Juliet-sleeved gown, along with a refined necklace similar to the one her eldest sister wore at the same event. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

Personality

Momoze projected the image of a calm and gentle girl. She did not appear to resent her mother's harsh words towards her or to fear for her own safety. She pitied her mother and brother, Marayam, for their inability to become Kings. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Momoze is first seen among Kakin's fourteen princes, seen sewing in her first appearance. She is one of six princes to put up listings on Kakin's website. Hanzo applies to her listing and is hired to become her bodyguard for the trip to the Dark Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 

During the eve (of the voyage) festival, Momoze stands with her siblings and Beyond on stage and waves to the crowd. Kakin's Royal Family is flown into the Black Whale shortly after. Momoze is next seen attending a banquet taking place that same day in the Black Whale, dressed in a light-colored dress, speaking to other attendees and standing near her younger brother Marayam. 

On the first day of the voyage, Momoze attends a lunch banquet with her siblings, wearing opera gloves and extravagant jewelry. Unbeknownst to her, her Guardian Spirit Beast attacks Prince Woble's bodyguards, taking control of one to kill three before being restrained and arrested. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 It is unclear whether Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast attacked Woble's bodyguards after she returned from the ceremony, or while she was there. Some points suggest it was after she returned, but nothing has been specified. 

After Momoze and Marayam return from the ceremony, their mother, Queen Sevanti, goes to Room 1012, bringing Marayam with her. Sevanti has all but six guards from Momoze's team who have moved to Marayam's escort, citing the importance of guarding Marayam. Hanzo watches Momoze as she knits in a rocking chair within earshot of her mother. One of Momoze's bodyguards mentions that their contract is with Momoze, and Momoze's servants express hesitation at the servants being moved too. Still, they all defer to Sevanti and comply after Momoze reassures them not to worry about her. Momoze allows two of her remaining guards to rest, but they thank her for her kindness and decline. Momoze accepts and thanks them in turn. She wonders why she feels tired while pitying her mother and brother, judging their fear as a sign that Marayam is not fit to be king. Unbeknownst to her, Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast sits behind her back, the source of her fatigue. 

A few hours later, at roughly 4 p.m. that day, Momoze lies fast asleep in her bed. Outside Room 1012's master bedroom, four of her remaining bodyguards stand watch. Her six bodyguards each silently keep watch for opportunities to assassinate her, hoping to do so in a manner that leaves proof of their perpetration without getting caught. As Momoze sleeps, her Guardian Spirit Beast silently fades away. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Later that day, one of Momoze's guards makes his way into her room and strangles her. By chance, Oito sees the act happening through a cockroach she controls with Little Eye and informs her guards. Babimyna uses his authority as 1 st Prince Benjamin's soldier so Room 1014 can contact Room 1012, but Room 1014's occupants are too late to prevent Momoze's death. Kakin's military removed Momoze's body. After hearing that four of the six guards had matching testimonies (the remaining two being on break), Sevanti reported the guards to Nasubi for collusion, and the six guards were taken into custody to face a court-martial. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 

In Room 1014, Sakata takes Kurapika aside to ask why Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast failed to protect her. From her unusual sleep schedule, Kurapika (correctly) guesses that one of the beasts that attacked Room 1014 was Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast, expending a considerable amount of her aura, allowing Bill to speculate that the criminal was familiar with Nen. 

Around the same time, Hanzo, in Room 1013's break room, learns of the death of his former employer and feels anger and regret at not being there to stop it. Biscuit tries to console him, but he ignores her consolation and uses her as a sounding board to identify that the culprit used a doppelgänger and was one of the two guards on break. Confident that the culprit will strike again, Hanzo prepares to stop him before he strikes again and swears to make him pay. 

The next day, King Nasubi pays his respects to Momoze. He stands and looks at her casket as Nugui offers his condolences. Nasubi tells him that her death is nothing to be sad about, that Momoze would become the foundation for the Tree of Kakin, and is shining even brighter than in life. Momoze lies in one of fourteen caskets arranged in a circle in a mysterious room with Nen-like inscriptions, with a fire lit next to the head of her casket. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

In Room 1013 on the same day, during their shift guarding Marayam, Hanzo and Biscuit agree that Marayam's dragon-like Guardian Spirit Beast has gradually become fiercer and bulked up. Hanzo guesses that Momoze's death had accelerated the change, implying that Marayam was aware of her death despite not being told about it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

At 10 a.m., the next pair of guards come to take over their duties, and Hanzo and Biscuit retire to Room 1013's break room. Hanzo entrusts his body to Biscuit to ensure no one interrupts his ability by talking to or touching his body and activates Hanzo Skill 4. He passes a sleeping guard and infiltrates Tuffdy's cell. Hanzo wakes Tuffdy up and tricks him into confessing to the murder and explaining his ability before strangling Tuffdy to avenge Momoze. 

Later, when Kurapika is explaining Manipulation to Yuhirai and Sakata, it's revealed that Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast was a pseudo-coercive type Manipulator, taking physical freedom from the target or driving them into a situation where they have no choice. 

On the morning of the third day, 9 th Prince Halkenburg and his men have a moment of silence in Room 1009 for Momoze. This moment of silence generates a massive amount of aura, shocking Shikaku into contacting Benjamin about it. Halkenburg is moved to tears by Momoze's death and begins his daily attempts to visit Nasubi and stop the succession war before it takes another victim. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

In Camilla's confinement residence in the VVIP area on the ninth day, Sarahell addresses the other Have-Nots and has Nukuocon (formerly cursing 8 th Prince Salé-salé) and Cavic (formerly cursing Momoze) investigate whether there are exorcists among the enemy. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Momoze's Employees

Abilities & Powers

According to 5 th Prince Tubeppa, owing to Momoze's position as a younger prince, she had no real political influence or power despite being a member of Kakin's royal family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 She had an interest in knitting. 

Guardian Spirit Beast

Like the rest of her siblings, Momoze received an egg from which a Guardian Spirit Beast, a variety of Nen beast, hatched to protect her. As a parasitic-type ability, she has no control over it, and it fades away as Momoze runs out of aura. The Guardian Spirit Beast is made out of aura, thus only Nen users can see and hear it. Momoze herself is unaware of its existence. Like all Guardian Spirit Beasts, Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast is subjected to two rule-like instincts: it is unable to fight other Guardian Spirit Beasts as well as to directly attack the host of one. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Momoze is the first of King Nasubi's children to die in the Succession War, dying in the evening of the first day of the voyage. 
Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast is the most cartoon-like of all Guardian Spirit Beasts revealed thus far.
